title: 2026 Oscar Best Picture Odds & Predictions
text: One Battle After Another leads at 75% to win Best Picture, followed by Sinners at 22%. Long-shots include Hamnet at 3% and Marty Supreme at 2%. The favorite has dominated precursor awards, winning at both the Producers Guild Awards and Directors Guild Awards, the two strongest predictors of Best Picture outcomes. As noted, The PGA's top prize has gone to 17 of the last 25 Best Picture winners, while the DGA is 16-for-25. However, Sinners gained momentum after winning the Screen Actors Guild award, the only major precursor it captured. This mirrors rare historical upsets: only three times since 2000 has the Best Picture winner relied solely on an SAG precursor win. Collin Whitchurch predicts One Battle After Another will prevail but acknowledges the emerging Sinners momentum. He notes the betting value appears poor at current levels, citing his own position at +400 odds from weeks prior. His recommendation: Sinners (+400 or better) represents the best value proposition. The consensus heavily favors the PGA/DGA winner, though recent SAG momentum warrants monitoring.
